channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta wants everyone to know that it, too, is investing a lot in AI
item.description: Not to be outdone by its close rival OpenAI, Meta has announced its plans to spend $60 to $65 billion on AI infrastructure this year, and is building a data center almost as big as Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New federal CIO onboard at OMB
item.description: Greg Barbaccia takes over for Clare Martorana as the government’s lead technology executive. The post New federal CIO onboard at OMB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s move to lift Biden-era AI rules sparks debate over fast-tracked advances
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order removing Biden-Administration rules governing AI development is being cast as an opening of AI development flood gates, which could fast track advances in the still-new technology, but could also pose risks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The New York State Senate Bill: Tackling 3D-Printed Gun Violence
item.description: New York state senate evaluates a bill that would change the 3D printing market, requiring background checks and criminal history for certain sales.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: FBI claims North Korean workers are hacking the US companies which hired them
item.description: Advice to counter state-backed threat actors turning on your business from within is also just generally sound recruiting advice.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 8: latest rumors, updates, and what we want to see
item.description: Samsung is thought to be working on another smartwatch for 2025 – here's what we think we know so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sony's next flagship headphones leak in official docs, and now I'm getting excited
item.description: An FCC filing for the Sony WH-1000XM6 shows better Bluetooth and what looks like different construction too
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A Pixel-inspired productivity upgrade for any Android device
item.description: My friend and fellow Android appreciator, allow me to shower you with two delightfully simultaneous truths from here in the land o’ Googley matters: I love using Google’s Pixel devices in part because of all the genuinely useful features they add into the Android equation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EUSPA adjudica a GMV la nueva versión del generador de datos del servicio HAS de Galileo
item.description: La Agencia de la Unión Europea para el Programa Espacial (EUSPA) ha adjudicado a la multinacional tecnológica GMV el desarrollo de una nueva versión del generador de datos de alta precisión (HADG, por sus siglas en inglés) del servicio HAS de Galileo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los líderes empresariales prevén centrar la inversión en innovación, eficiencia y resiliencia
item.description: En un contexto de continuada incertidumbre en el mercado global, los líderes empresariales parecen haber arrancado el año con una dosis extra de positividad respecto a las perspectivas de su organización.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AMD’s unpatched chip microcode glitch may require extreme measures by CISOs
item.description: Due to a partner disclosure, AMD has found itself in the unenviable position of having to confirm the existence of a major cybersecurity problem in its chip microcode before it could post a fix. But that headache is mild compared to those of their customers’ CISOs.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: The Oval Office ‘Stargate Project’ reveal was just more tech industry genuflecting
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: What to do when you can’t escape your old boss on social media
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FinOps automation: Raising the bar on lowering cloud costs
item.description: IT professionals have been striving to manage cloud costs effectively since the inception of cloud computing. Those efforts have evolved into FinOps, a business discipline and a set of best practices to optimize cloud spending.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google BigQuery gets metadata service with Iceberg support
item.description: Google Cloud is adding a new metadata service that is compatible with Apache Iceberg to its managed data analytics service BigQuery in order to help enterprises cut down complexities around metadata management.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Outlook for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: There are countless ways to communicate electronically, including texting, social media, chat apps, team task managers, and videoconferencing software. Given the myriad ways you can get in touch with others, you may well think email is dead. Think again.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI at Work: How SOS 102 is Building Safer Communities in Kazakhstan
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to stop robocalls on your flip phone
item.description: Flip phones are harder to block spam calls from, but it's not impossible. Kurt the "CyberGuy" explains how to stop incoming robocalls on your flip phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
